result:
1. multi.exec return shape bug — Section 4 snippet — `const [count] = await multi.exec()` — in ioredis/node-redis, `exec()` returns `[[err, incrResult], [err, expireResult]]`, so `count` is the tuple `[null, N]`, not `N`. Truthy comparison `count > limit` is always truthy/NaN → rate limiter is effectively bypassed. Fix: `const res = await multi.exec(); const count = res[0][1]` (ioredis) or destructure `[[,count]]`.

2. Region prefix missing from documented key — Section 4 — key format `rl:{ip}:{route_prefix}:{minute_bucket}` has no region, but Section 10 says 2025-12 SEV2 RCA added region prefix. Handbook contradicts its own incident history; either fix did not ship or doc drifted. Recurrence of global-collapse for one hot IP. Fix: `rl:{region}:{ip}:{route_prefix}:{minute_bucket}` and regression test.

3. Fixed bucket, not sliding — Section 4 — `Math.floor(Date.now()/60000)` is fixed minute bucket; text claims "sliding-window approximation". Allows 2× burst at bucket boundary (limit worth of traffic in last second + limit in first second). Fix: either rename to "fixed-window" or implement actual sliding window (e.g., weighted prev+curr bucket).

4. rate_limit runs before auth_jwt — Section 3 pipeline steps 3 vs 5 — throttle keyed only by IP, so CGNAT/corporate-NAT users collide and share a bucket, while authenticated heavy users get no per-user limit. Also means unauth attackers and auth users share the same bucket dimension. Fix: two-stage — cheap per-IP pre-auth cap, per-`sub` cap post-auth.

5. Auto-rollback conflicts with security forensic rule — Section 6 "SEV1 ANY HOUR triggers automatic rollback" vs Section 8 "DO NOT roll back without coordination — security may want to capture forensic state". On a security SEV1 the automation destroys the evidence before responders arrive. Fix: exclude security-tagged alerts from auto-rollback, require `@security-oncall` ack.

6. body_size_guard after rate_limit — Section 3 steps 3 → 4 — oversized bodies (`>2MB`) consume the rate-limit counter before being `413`'d, and depending on `express.json` position may already be buffered into memory. DoS amplification and wasted quota. Fix: Content-Length precheck in `header_sanitize` or move `body_size_guard` to step 2.5; ensure body parser is mounted after the guard.

7. JWKS stale-while-revalidate window re-exposes 2025-06 — Section 2 rule 4 / Section 5 — 15min refresh + 16min soft TTL + SWR means a poisoned key set (the 2025-06 SEV1 pattern) persists serving for up to ~16min after auth-svc recovers. RCA "sanity-check on refresh" is not described in the handbook itself. Fix: document the sanity check (expected kid set, signature self-test), shorten SWR grace, add kill-switch config to force cache flush.

8. `jti` not deduplicated at edge — Section 5 — explicit tradeoff, but combined with 30s clock skew it permits at least a 30s replay window for leaked tokens against any downstream that also does not dedupe. Fix: bounded `jti` seen-set in Redis with TTL = token lifetime + skew, at least for mutating routes.

9. Trust-boundary header list is incomplete — Section 2 rule 1 / Section 5 — strips `X-User-Id`, `X-Org-Id`, `X-Forwarded-For`, `X-Real-IP`, `X-Original-Authorization`; no mention of RFC 7239 `Forwarded`, `CF-Connecting-IP`, `True-Client-IP`, or the `X-Internal-*` set (which Section 5 claims is stripped but rule 1 does not enumerate). Spoof path if any downstream or middleware consults an un-stripped alias. Fix: allowlist (not denylist) — drop everything not in a known-safe set, explicitly including `X-Internal-*`.

10. PII redaction scope is log-only — Section 2 rule 5 / Section 7 — redaction runs in the log-emit middleware, but OTel traces at 100% for `5xx` and `always_trace_routes.json` can carry request attributes (`http.url`, `http.request.body`, user headers) to Tempo with no redaction mentioned. `pii-leak-detector` only runs on logs. Fix: apply `pii_redact` to span attributes before export; extend detector to Tempo.

11. Prometheus `{route}` label cardinality — Section 7 — if `route` is the matched URL (e.g. `/users/:id/orders/:oid`) cardinality is bounded; if it is the raw path it explodes. Not specified. Fix: pin to the Express route template, reject unknown routes with a bucketed `other` label.

12. API-key revocation lag — Section 5 — 30s LRU on last-100k keys means a revoked key is accepted for up to 30s across every edge pod. Acceptable for rotation, risky for compromise. Fix: pub/sub invalidation channel from `auth-svc` that evicts on revoke events, with the 30s TTL as fallback.
